https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/r.commit_message:
Bug 1981748 - Avoid building the top layer past the 255th page. r=dshin

This patch makes sure that top layer display items that we build for a
given page are uniquely tagged with the page number, which avoids
uniqueness assertion failures with the patch from bug 1972694.

In theory this could also trivially happen by printing a modal <dialog>
or so, but it currently doesn't, see bug 1981732.

The patch in bug 1972694 exposes this on a devtools test
(browser_browser_toolbox_print_preview.js) because we start creating an
isolation container for the DevTools anonymous content, which is in the
top layer, and we currently didn't set the page number in the
ViewportFrame::BuildDisplayList which ends up building it, failing our
unique display item assertions.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D260270

ranked_scenarios.summary: Don't build duplicate display items for pagination

description.text:
We generally avoid creating duplicate items, since we need to uniquely identify them for retained display lists.

With the frame tree for paginated content, we can end up with an OOF frame being part of one page, but the placeholder for it on a different page. Since we have to descend through the placeholder to build display items for the OOF frame, we can end up needing to DL build though multiple pages (see BuildDisplayListForExtraPage) to create the content for a single page. When we're displaying multiple pages (for print preview), this can result in duplicate items.

Example frame tree:

```
Page1
    Block1
    AbsoluteList
        Block2
Page2
    Block3 (opacity: 0.5)
        Block4
        Placeholder(Block2)
```

DL:

```
Transform(Page1)
    BackgroundColor(Block1)
    Opacity(Block3)
        BackgroundColor(Block2)
Transform(Page2)
    Opacity(Block3)
        BackgroundColor(Block4)
```

In this case the opacity item for Block3 is duplicated, since it contains content belonging to both pages.

I think we can tag items that are built from extra pages with the page index of the destination page (tag the first Opacity with page 1) and use that index in the unique key computation.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Hit testing on fission iframes in print preview is messed up.

description.text: Because of the custom transforms stuff.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Investigate implementation of fallback "slicing" fragmentation using display list

description.text:
Mats has proposed an approach to a fallback fragmentation strategy using display lists, which may be "good enough" in many cases: https://gist.github.com/MatsPalmgren/8eb65464f08850597877ab155e856a0c

This bug is for tracking work to investigate and potentially implement this idea.

The nsDisplayListBuilder structure from layout/painting/nsDisplayList.h uses extra padding, as reported by clang static analyzer.

Reorder fields to optimize memory usage.
This split is not very useful and complicates the code. I also looked into removing `nsDisplayItemLink` but it would require rewriting the linked list code in `nsDisplayList`.
